# Climate Change Adaptation, Drought, Flood Management
# Generated by KrishiMitra AI Knowledge Base Builder
# Source: ICAR guidelines + agricultural research literature
# Last updated: 2024-25 season

# Comprehensive Guide on Climate-Smart Agriculture for Indian Farmers

## Introduction
Climate-smart agriculture (CSA) is a strategic approach that aims to enhance food security in the face of climate change while reducing greenhouse gas emissions. This guide provides specific recommendations and practices for Indian farmers to manage drought, flood, heat stress, unseasonal rain, and leverage carbon credit opportunities.

---

### 1. Drought Management

#### Early Warning System
- **IMD Meghdoot App**: Utilize the IMD Meghdoot app for real-time weather updates.
- **5-Day Forecast Check**: Review the 5-day forecast before initiating any farming operations to avoid water wastage and ensure timely planting.

#### Drought Tolerant Varieties
- **Wheat**: GW-496 (ICAR/CIB&RC approved)
- **Rice**: Sahbhagi Dhan (ICAR/CIB&RC approved)
- **Maize**: African Tall (ICAR/CIB&RC approved)

#### Moisture Conservation Practices
- **Mulching with Crop Residue**: Apply 20 cm of crop residue mulch to reduce evaporation by 30%.
- **In-Situ Moisture Conservation**:
  - **Contour Bunding**: Create bunds along the contours of the field to retain water and prevent soil erosion.
  - **Tied Ridges**: Construct ridges with tied straw or other organic materials to enhance water retention.

#### Contingency Crops
- **Bajra**: Plant by July 15 if monsoon fails.
- **Moong**: Suitable for early planting in dry conditions.
- **Sunhemp**: A fast-growing legume that can be planted as a cover crop or green manure.

#### National Drought Monitoring Centre (NDMC)
- **Website**: [nmsc.gov.in](http://nmsc.gov.in) for real-time drought status and advisories.

---

### 2. Flood and Waterlogging Management

#### Flood Tolerant Varieties
- **Wheat**: Swarna Sub1 (ICAR/CIB&RC approved, tolerant to 7 days of submergence)
- **Rice**: FR-13A gene (ICAR/CIB&RC approved)

#### Post-Flood Recovery Practices
- **Immediate Water Removal**: Drain standing water from the field as soon as possible.
- **Foliar Spray with Urea**: Apply a 2% urea solution to promote plant recovery and growth.
- **Fungicide Application**: Use Carbendazim (0.1 kg/ha) to prevent fungal outbreaks.

#### Soil Rehabilitation
- **Gypsum Application**: Add 500 kg/ha of gypsum to improve soil structure and drainage.
- **Organic Matter Addition**: Incorporate well-composted organic matter at a rate of 2-3 t/ha to enhance soil health and water retention.

---

### 3. Heat Stress Management

#### Wheat
- **Sowing Time**: Sow wheat early in November (November 1-15).
- **Heat Tolerant Varieties**:
  - K-9107
  - NW-1014
- **Foliar Sprays**:
  - Salicylic Acid: Apply at a concentration of 1 mM.
  - Thiourea: Apply at a concentration of 1000 ppm.

#### Night Irrigation
- **Timing**: Provide night irrigation when ambient temperature exceeds 38°C to reduce heat stress on crops.

---

### 4. Unseasonal Rain Management

#### Harvest Timing
- **Early Harvesting**: Harvest crops when they reach 85% maturity.
- **Desiccant Application**:
  - Diquat 20SL: Apply at a rate of 0.5 L/ha to accelerate drying in wheat.

#### Post-Harvest Storage
- **Temporary Storage**: Store harvested grains under tarpaulin covers to protect from rain and ensure quality.
- **PMFBY Coverage**: Utilize the Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY) for insurance coverage against post-harvest losses.

---

### 5. Carbon Credit Opportunities

#### Practices
- **Zero Tillage**: Implement zero tillage to reduce carbon emissions and improve soil health.
- **Agroforestry**: Integrate trees into farming systems to sequester carbon.
- **SRI Paddy**: Adopt the System of Rice Intensification (SRI) for sustainable rice cultivation.

#### Carbon Credit Registries
- **Verra Registry**: [verra.org](https://www.verra.org/)
- **Gold Standard Registry**: [goldstandard.org](https://www.goldstandard.org/)

#### Farmer Participation
- **FPO Aggregation**: Join farmer producer organizations (FPOs) to aggregate small farmers and facilitate participation in carbon credit programs.

---

### Economic Threshold Levels (ETL)
- **Pest Management**:
  - **Wheat Stem Rust**: ETL = 10% infected plants.
  - **Rice Blast**: ETL = 5% infected panicles.
  - **Maize Lepidoptera**: ETL = 20 larvae per plant.

### Fertilizer Split Schedule
- **Wheat**:
  - Basal: Urea (100 kg/ha), DAP (75 kg/ha)
  - Top-dressing: Urea (50 kg/ha) at tillering stage, Urea (25 kg/ha) at jointing stage.
  
- **Rice**:
  - Basal: Urea (100 kg/ha), DAP (75 kg/ha)
  - Mid-season: Urea (50 kg/ha)
  - Top-dressing: Urea (25 kg/ha) at panicle initiation stage.

### MSP 2024-25 Values
- **Wheat**: ₹1,960 per quintal
- **Rice**: ₹1,738 per quintal

---

This guide provides a structured approach to implementing climate-smart agriculture practices in India. By following these recommendations, farmers can enhance their resilience to climate variability and improve overall productivity and sustainability.
